segments.text: I will watch my ways, so that I don’t sin with my tongue. I will keep my mouth with a bridle while the wicked is before me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I was mute with silence. I held my peace, even from good.
My heart was hot within me. While I meditated, the fire burned. I spoke with my tongue:

Yahweh, show me my end, 
what is the measure of my days. 
Let me know how frail I am.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Behold, you have made my days hand widths. My lifetime is as nothing before you. Surely every man stands as a breath.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text:
Surely every man walks like a shadow. Surely they busy themselves in vain. 
He heaps up, and doesn’t know who shall gather.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now, Lord, what do I wait for? My hope is in you.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Deliver me from all my transgressions. Don’t make me the reproach of the foolish.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text:
I was mute. I didn’t open my mouth, 
because you did it.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Remove your scourge away from me. I am overcome by the blow of your hand.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When you rebuke and correct man for iniquity, you consume his wealth like a moth. Surely every man is but a breath.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Hear my prayer, Yahweh, and give ear to my cry. Don’t be silent at my tears. For I am a stranger with you, a foreigner, as all my fathers were.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Oh spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go away and exist no more.
segments.speaker: David
